unreleasedSourceNotes: Source-only after 0.6.34: standalone Stem WAV export now builds one ZIP with audio/stems WAVs, manifests/stem-wavs-manifest.json, deterministic paths and byte-size summaries; installed-app smoke is still required before release claims.

Source-only after 0.6.34: standalone Section Loop export now builds one ZIP with audio/sections WAVs, manifests/section-loops-manifest.json, deterministic paths and byte-size summaries; installed-app smoke is still required before release claims.

Source-only after 0.6.34: stem, section-loop, Godot and web-game ZIP exports now use an installed-app native Save As destination with browser download fallback; installed-app smoke is still required before release claims.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated-section clips now expose undoable transpose, gain and Drums/Bass/Chords/Melody/Guitar stem-mute controls that affect playback/export without mutating Chordsmith source data; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: first generated Drums branch-view foundation can create/collapse Kick, Snare and Hi-hat source-view tracks, with branch Volume/Pan/Mute proxied to drum-lane metadata and Solo guarded until lane-solo render support exists; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: selected audio clips now expose undoable non-destructive Duration editing in the inspector, feeding the same source-safe audio-region render path as gain, fades and source offset; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: media relink and fresh reload analysis now clear stale source-derived waveform/native-decoder/decoded-cache/transient metadata while preserving user annotations, so old peaks, markers or decoded WAV caches are not advertised for a newly linked or reloaded source; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: browser runtime-only audio remains non-reloadable/non-collectable, but now exposes Relink so installed-app users can repair saved projects by attaching a durable native source; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: selected audio clips now have an undoable Analyze action that writes source-preserving transient marker metadata from waveform peaks and surfaces marker counts in Media Pool, About/Diagnostics and feedback summaries, with transient markers drawn over media waveform previews; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: selected audio clips now have an undoable source-safe Reverse action that is carried through WebAudio playback, offline WAV render, native runtime-audio regions and the native renderer cache contract without mutating source media; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Full Song WAV export now uses the full-song export profile sample rate for browser and native offline renders, Stem WAV and Section Loop ZIP renders carry their own profile sample rates into the shared WAV renderer, and the Export dock exposes sample-rate/tail controls while export-profile validation rejects unsupported WAV bit-depth/sample-rate settings instead of producing mislabeled 16-bit files; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: track sends now support real pre-fader routing in WebAudio playback, browser offline WAV render and the native renderer by splitting track input from fader gain, with diagnostics/manifests counting pre-fader sends without planned-mode warnings; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated drum branch tracks now support real branch Solo by persisting drum-lane solo metadata, filtering generated drum events to soloed lanes, enabling branch Solo buttons, and invalidating native drum stem cache signatures when lane solo changes; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated drums now expose double-click branch entry points on the Drums track header, inline drum section surface and generated drum clip surface, all routed through the same undoable Branch Drums command path; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: stem and game-pack export plans are now branch-aware for generated drum source views, adding deterministic shared-kit drum branch stems and rendering each branch stem by keeping the parent Drums source audible while soloing the matching drum lane; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: visible generated drum branch tracks now receive live meter taps from their matching parent drum-lane events, so shared-kit branch meters move alongside the parent Drums meter during WebAudio and native playback; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated drums now support right-click/context-menu branching on the Drums track header and generated drum clip surfaces, sharing the same undoable Branch Drums command path as the button and double-click entry points; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: visible generated drum branch lanes now own their generated event destinations so branch output routing, FX and sends affect WebAudio playback, browser offline WAV render and native playback payloads while parent Drums continues to represent the overall kit meter/source view; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated Drums branch views now cover the full shared kit lane surface (Kick, Snare, Clap, Hi-hat, Open Hat, Low/Mid/High Tom, Crash and Ride) with deterministic branch tracks and stem paths, while original Chordsmith source grids remain preserved as Kick/Snare/Hi-hat; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: visible generated Drums branch views now route existing Chordsmith level-2 Snare and Hi-hat source accents into Clap and Open Hat branch events/stems without changing unbranched Pocket Audio Core parity or the original source grid; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated Drums branch rows now carry stable drum branch group/lane data attributes and a subtle grouped timeline treatment so the full shared-kit branch surface behaves like one drum-family view instead of unrelated generated tracks; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated Drums branch views now support an undoable Hide/Show Branch Rows command that persists in project metadata and hides the timeline branch group without deleting branch tracks, routing, sends, FX or export stems; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: generated Drums branch rows now provide DAW-only source overlays for live-kit lanes such as Low/Mid/High Tom, Crash and Ride, with undoable step editing, save/reopen persistence, playback/render events and branch stem export without mutating preserved Chordsmith source grids; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: the file-first MCP command path now supports branching generated drums and cycling DAW-only branch overlay steps for the full shared drum-lane set, so Codex/manual smoke can create Tom/Crash/Ride-style branch hits through the same app command layer instead of broad UI automation; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: drum-lane mix metadata now includes a first-class Gate control that scales generated drum event durations, appears in the Drum Kit Lanes UI, flows through WebAudio/offline/native event payloads, participates in native drum stem cache signatures, and can be set through file-first MCP lane commands; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: timeline markers now support first-class game-state cue values (calm, danger, combat, win, lose and menu) through schema/migration, timeline UI, undoable command paths, file-first MCP and Godot/Web game-pack manifests; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: live audio tracks now persist an explicit mono/stereo recording channel mode, expose it in the mixer/details UI, pass it into native CPAL recording, write matching mono/stereo WAV files, and stamp native takes with grouping/channel metadata; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: playback/export hydration from original source paths or project-relative decoded native cache now applies the same media repair metadata quietly without adding undo history, clearing missing/unresolved flags and recording reload source evidence when recovery succeeds; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: project-relative media/cache path handling now normalizes slashes and project://media/ forms, rejects traversal/absolute/scheme paths for native reload candidates, warns in project diagnostics when saved refs are unsafe, and falls back to a safe project-media/ target if native collection reports an unsafe relative path; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Stem ZIP, Section Loop ZIP, Godot and Web game-pack manifests now include path-sanitized media portability counts and warn when media must be collected or relinked before the embedded source .pocketdaw is portable; the Export Details dock now exposes the same sanitized portability counts before export; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: placed audio clips now preserve durable recording/take-lane metadata aliases, same-track grouped takes can be activated, archived/restored without deleting media, and split into first comp-from-playhead segments through undoable audio-inspector actions and file-first MCP commands; About/Diagnostics, feedback payloads and MCP project summaries expose grouped-take counts/statuses for smoke observation; this is a foundation only, not a full punch/comping/take-lane release claim, and installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: modal panels, including About / Diagnostics, are offset below the menu and transport chrome with scrollable viewport bounds so the close control stays reachable in constrained desktop windows; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: collected project-media paths are normalized to project-relative forward-slash refs in native collect results and saved media-pool metadata, reducing moved/zipped project path drift; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: timeline edit ranges are now first-class project data with toolbar/menu/inspector controls and file-first MCP commands to range a selected clip, copy the loop into the edit range, clear it, render a range overlay, split generated/audio clips at range boundaries, crop the selected generated/audio clip to the range, delete the selected clip's range while preserving outside material, ripple-delete the selected clip range on the same track, ripple-delete the edit range across all tracks for source-safe generated/audio clips, preserve audio source offsets, and expose the active range in MCP project summaries; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI split/crop/delete/ripple range edits now route through the same edit-range command/MCP paths as audio/generated clips while trimming overlapping notes, retaining surviving CC/program/pitch-bend/aftertouch data with shifted ticks, closing same-track gaps for selected MIDI ripple deletes, and participating in all-track Ripple All without double-moving MIDI clips; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI import now preserves richer DAW-useful metadata from real files, including tempo events, time-signature events, key signatures, lyrics, SysEx counts and track summaries, so future Chordsmith-style interpretation can be optional without weakening raw MIDI clip preservation; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI import placement can now keep the compatible single-clip import or split raw MIDI into one clip/track per source track or per MIDI channel while sharing one source media-pool item and preserving notes, CCs, program changes, pitch bends and aftertouch through MIDI export; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Add Track now includes a first-class MIDI Instrument track and the Piano Roll can create an empty MIDI clip at the playhead for direct MIDI authoring without importing a file; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI import placement now includes a raw drum-channel split mode that separates channel-10 notes into pitch-named MIDI clips/tracks while preserving non-drum channels as ordinary MIDI instead of converting or mutating Chordsmith/generated drum source; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI export now writes DAW track names as standard SMF track-name metadata on musical tracks, improving interchange for imported source-track/channel/drum-split MIDI; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: MIDI import now records clear warnings when files contain multiple tempo or time-signature events, preserving those maps as metadata while stating that playback/export still use the project tempo and meter until DAW tempo/meter lanes exist; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Media Pool cards now surface MIDI import warnings from saved metadata, including tempo/meter map warnings after reopen, with escaped rendering for untrusted media metadata; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll now exposes an undoable MIDI clip bar-length control so direct-authored MIDI clips can be extended or shortened without deleting note/controller metadata; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll Add Note now places notes relative to the selected MIDI clip start instead of project bar 1, matching controller insertion and preventing late-starting MIDI clips from receiving far-future note ticks; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Game-pack ZIP exports now have an automated verifier command for Godot/Web pack structure, manifest path coverage, artifact byte summaries, WAV-only current metadata, planned codec guardrails and unsafe path rejection; target-runtime Godot/Web smoke remains manual before release claims.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Direct MIDI authoring now extends a MIDI clip to the next quarter-bar when new notes or CC points are added beyond the current clip end, preventing freshly authored events from being stored outside the playable/exported range; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Godot game-pack export now has a DAW-side local loopback push bridge and Push Godot Pack UI action that attempts a localhost receiver first, then falls back to the existing ZIP Save As/download path when unavailable; no Godot addon code was changed and target-runtime smoke remains manual before release claims.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Godot/Web game-pack delivery targets are now first-class export metadata surfaced in Export Details and MCP export plans, including verifier commands, WAV-only support and manual target-smoke boundaries; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Full Song WAV export profiles now support explicit Stereo or Mono channel mode in the Export Details dock, with browser and native full-song WAV renders producing 16-bit PCM files in the requested channel layout; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Stem WAV ZIP and Section Loop ZIP profiles now expose sample-rate and Stereo/Mono channel-mode controls in Export Details, their browser/native render projects and manifest audio metadata honor those profile settings, and game-pack manifests now report the actual full-song WAV profile sample rate instead of the project default; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: WAV export profiles now expose explicit Off/Peak normalization controls in Export Details; browser WAV encoding and native PCM16 WAV post-processing apply peak normalization only when the active WAV profile requests it, WAV ZIP/game-pack manifests record the requested normalization mode/target, and freeze/game-pack renders keep normalization disabled unless their own render profile enables it; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll clip-level pitch transforms now include semitone up/down alongside octave up/down, using the same undoable MIDI command path, metadata persistence and MIDI-safe pitch clamping as existing pitch transforms; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Generated-pattern clips now participate in the same source-preserving edit-range operations as generated-section clips, including Split Range, Crop Range, Delete Range, same-track Ripple Delete and all-track Ripple All while preserving pattern metadata and sourceStartBar windows; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll now exposes editable MIDI program-change rows with undoable add/edit/duplicate/delete commands, save/reopen persistence and MIDI export preservation; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll now exposes editable MIDI pitch-bend rows with undoable add/edit/duplicate/delete commands, save/reopen persistence and MIDI export preservation; edited CC/program/pitch-bend ticks extend clips when needed so authored expression events stay playable/exportable; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Piano Roll now exposes editable MIDI aftertouch rows with undoable add/edit/duplicate/delete commands, save/reopen persistence and MIDI export preservation, completing the first editable MIDI expression lane set across CC, program, pitch-bend and aftertouch metadata; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Selected MIDI clips can now map General MIDI drum notes into generated Drums branch overlays via the Piano Roll, undoable command path and file-first MCP command, using the current Chordsmith drum-import note mapping idea while preserving the original MIDI clip and Chordsmith source grids; installed-app smoke is still required.

Source-only after 0.6.34: Selected MIDI clips can now map non-drum MIDI notes into DAW-only generated Melody overlays via the Piano Roll, undoable command path and file-first MCP command, preserving the raw MIDI clip and Chordsmith source grids while letting generated-section playback/render use the interpreted melody layer; installed-app smoke is still required.

notes: Pocket DAW 0.6.34 was installed locally from the staged itch installer artifact and launched from C:\Users\sam_s\AppData\Local\Pocket DAW\pocket-daw.exe.

The staged installer SHA-256 matched releases/itch/pocket-daw-release-manifest-v0.6.34.json. The manifest records gitCommitSha 6df2186334b365c3499616d69f86372c2f019e29 and dirtyWorkingTree true because the 0.6.34 bass-renderer/version/status changes had not yet been committed when this local tester artifact was built.
MCP live bridge reported Pocket DAW project/app version 0.6.34, schema 2, and loaded C:\Users\sam_s\Music\imported-chordsmith-project test.pocketdaw.

The native render contract invalidated the old generated-stem cache: MCP reported 70 stale source hashes, rebuilt 70 generated regions, and finished with full native cache coverage and no generated stem render failures.
User live-listening smoke confirmed the bass sounds better in the installed app after the Chordsmith-style harmonic low-pass and release-tail native renderer fix.
Playback/performance smoke ran through native-cpal with no scheduler misses, no late/skipped events, no graph rebuilds, no native fallback, max native callback about 3.4 ms, and slowCallbackCount 0.
Pocket DAW 0.6.34 was published to GitHub release pocket-daw-v0.6.34 from clean source commit 1b89374ac9a7c53cca3ea936909db62984de9031 on 2026-06-27.

The public latest updater manifest reports version 0.6.34 and the downloaded setup EXE Pocket.DAW_0.6.34_x64-setup.exe matched SHA-256 89625636a3e68c9162e0dd3ea5a5f48f12673d2cfc439dab03134c6ddcb75f67.

The itch windows-installer channel now reports bootstrapper-0.6.34; a fetched channel copy contains index.html, README_FIRST.txt, CHECKSUMS_SHA256.txt, and Pocket_DAW_Itch_Bootstrapper_v0.6.34.exe so browser-mode itch requests no longer fail with missing index.html.
Sam manually installed the bootstrapper from itch on a laptop, used it to reinstall Pocket DAW, and confirmed it works great.
